title: Scene Beat Plan
description: Source-beat semantic plan layer for a scene. Describes the fine-grained narrative/semantic units (beats) within a scene, independent of final Kling shot durations. This is the input layer to the omni_clip_planner, which computes Kling-valid integer-second shots (3..15s) from these source beats. Schema version 0.x-draft; promoted to 1.0 after A3 feedback loop closes.

schema_version.description: Pre-1.0 draft. Promoted to 1.0 after A3 feedback loop closes.

source_beats.description: Array of semantic source beats describing the scene's narrative/visual progression. Each beat is a fine-grained unit that may later be merged into Kling-valid shots by omni_clip_planner. Do NOT assign duration_seconds here; durations are computed by the packer during A5/B6.

beat_id.description: Unique identifier for this beat within the scene (e.g., ESTABLISH_KITCHEN, DIALOGUE_NADIA_01, WRIST_INSERT_1).

content.description: Human-readable description of what happens in this beat. Example: 'Pan across kitchen counter, settling on Nadia's hands as she pours espresso.'

semantic_duration_hint.description: Semantic category guiding beat merging during packing: short_insert = sub-3s detail (eye movement, hand detail, object flourish); normal = standard beat (dialogue line, transition, action); long_hold = extended focus (establishing shot, meditation, sustained action). The packer uses this hint to decide merge-into-neighbor or standalone placement.

may_merge_with_next.description: True if this beat can be merged into the next beat during packing (preferred for short_insert and optional actions). False if this beat should preferably be its own shot or merged only as a last resort.

splittable.description: True if this beat can be split across multiple Kling shots during packing (e.g., a long sustained action or establishing shot). False if beat must remain atomic (e.g., dialogue punch-line, critical object reveal, narrative milestone).

narrative_role.description: Optional semantic role classification for the beat. establish = scene setting, context establishment; action = character physical action; dialogue = speech/character interaction; hold = sustained focus without action change; transition = location/subject shift; pulse_check = brief moment confirming emotional/thematic beat; insert = detail shot (hand, object, expression close-up).

required_element_ids.description: Optional: repo element IDs (e.g. C01, LOC001, PROP003) that must be present or visible in this beat. Operator-authored; never computed or guessed from text. Used by packer to propagate to manifest shots. Resolves ambiguous pronouns (She/He/Her) at the manifest layer.

figures.description: Distinct on-screen figures present in this beat. Each figure carries its own Kling @alias so multiple figures sharing a base character identity are never collapsed (anti-clone). Propagated to omni_clip_manifest shots by the packer; operator-authored.

notes.description: Optional human-readable notes, such as visual style cues, emotional intent, or special technical considerations.
Overall scene-level notes, such as mood, pacing constraints, or known dependencies on other scenes or elements.

created_by.description: User or agent that created this record.
